applicability.notes: Major Structural Inspection per Section 8.2.5. Required after major repairs, structural modifications, overload events, or accidents. Includes comprehensive structural examination, non-destructive testing where appropriate, and mandatory load testing.

fields.label: Inspector Certification/Qualification
fields.help_text: Major structural inspections require qualified inspector per Section 8.2.5

fields.label: Reason for Major Structural Inspection
fields.help_text: Select primary reason for this inspection

fields.label: Description of Work Performed
fields.help_text: Detailed description of repairs, modifications, or incident requiring this inspection

fields.label: Work Order/Repair Order Number
fields.help_text: Reference to work order or repair documentation

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

guidance: Review all documentation related to repairs or modifications performed
Verify repair procedures and methods were approved
Check welding procedures specification (WPS) if welding was performed
Verify welder certifications if welding was performed
Confirm replacement parts are OEM or approved equivalent
Verify engineering approval for modifications or non-standard repairs
Confirm manufacturer consultation if required per nature of work

fields.label: Repair procedures and methods reviewed and approved
Welding procedures specification (WPS) reviewed if welding performed
Welder certifications verified if welding performed
Replacement parts verified as OEM or approved equivalent
Engineering approval obtained for modifications or non-standard repairs
Manufacturer consulted if required per nature of work

defect.title: CRITICAL: Repair documentation incomplete or inadequate
defect.description: All repairs and modifications must be documented per Section 7.11 before unit can be returned to service. Missing or inadequate documentation found.

standard_text.excerpt: Review prior work documentation, maintenance records, and manufacturer bulletins. Verify all markings are legible and properly maintained.

guidance: Comprehensive inspection of all structural components per Section 8.2.5
Focus on repaired areas and adjacent structure
Look for cracks, deformation, corrosion, wear, and damage
Check for proper alignment and fit of components
Inspect boom base section including mounting points
Inspect all intermediate boom sections
Inspect outermost boom section and platform mounting
Inspect turret structure thoroughly
Inspect pedestal and mounting to vehicle chassis
Inspect platform frame, floor, railings, and gates
Inspect all outrigger beams and mounting structure

fields.label: Boom Base Section (Cracks, Deformation, Corrosion)
fields.help_text: Thoroughly inspect base section including mounting points

fields.label: Boom Mid Sections (All sections)
fields.help_text: Inspect all intermediate boom sections

fields.label: Boom Fly Section (Including platform attachment)
fields.help_text: Inspect outermost boom section and platform mounting

fields.label: Turret Structure and Frame
fields.help_text: Complete inspection of turret structure

fields.label: Pedestal and Mounting Structure
fields.help_text: Inspect pedestal and mounting to vehicle chassis

fields.label: Platform Structure (Frame, Floor, Railings, Gates)
fields.help_text: Complete platform structural inspection

fields.label: Outrigger/Stabilizer Structure
fields.help_text: Inspect all outrigger beams and mounting structure

fields.label: Structural Inspection Notes
fields.help_text: Document any areas of concern or observations

defect.title: CRITICAL: Structural defects found during major structural inspection
defect.description: Structural defects found that require correction before unit can be returned to service.

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

guidance: Comprehensive inspection of all welds per Section 8.2.5
Special attention to repaired welds and adjacent areas
Look for cracks, porosity, incomplete penetration, undercut, overlap, slag inclusion
NDT may be required for critical welds
Document exact location of all weld repairs
Inspect all accessible welds on boom structure
Inspect all turret and pedestal welds
Inspect platform frame and railing welds
Inspect outrigger structural welds

fields.label: Location of Repaired Welds
fields.help_text: Document exact location of all weld repairs

fields.label: Repaired Welds Visual Inspection
fields.help_text: Visual inspection of all repaired welds

fields.label: All Boom Section Welds
fields.help_text: Inspect all accessible welds on boom structure

fields.label: Turret and Pedestal Welds
fields.help_text: Inspect all turret and pedestal welds

fields.label: Platform Structural Welds
fields.help_text: Inspect platform frame and railing welds

fields.label: Outrigger/Stabilizer Welds
fields.help_text: Inspect outrigger structural welds

fields.label: Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) required?
fields.help_text: Critical welds may require NDT per Section 8.2.5

fields.label: NDT Method Used (if applicable)
fields.help_text: Select NDT method(s) performed

fields.label: NDT Results Summary
fields.help_text: Summary of NDT findings and test report references

defect.title: CRITICAL: Weld defects found during major structural inspection
defect.description: Weld defects found that require correction before unit can be returned to service.

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

steps.title: Bolts, Pins, and Fasteners Detailed Inspection

guidance: Detailed inspection of all bolts, pins, and fasteners
Special attention to critical mounting points
Verify correct grade bolts used per manufacturer specifications
Verify critical bolts torqued to specification
Check pedestal mounting bolts to chassis for tightness and condition
Check all turret bearing attachment bolts
Check all boom pivot pins for wear and security
Check all hydraulic cylinder mounting pins
Check platform attachment bolts and pins

fields.label: Location of Replaced Fasteners
fields.help_text: Document which fasteners were replaced

fields.label: Fastener grades verified per specifications?
fields.help_text: Verify correct grade bolts used per manufacturer specs

fields.label: Torque values verified per specifications?
fields.help_text: Critical bolts torqued to specification

fields.label: Pedestal Mounting Bolts
fields.help_text: Critical mounting bolts to chassis - check tightness and condition

fields.label: Turret Bearing Mounting Bolts
fields.help_text: Check all turret bearing attachment bolts

fields.label: Boom Pivot Pins and Retainers
fields.help_text: Check all boom pivot pins for wear and security

fields.label: Cylinder Mounting Pins and Hardware
fields.help_text: Check all hydraulic cylinder mounting pins

fields.label: Platform Mounting Hardware
fields.help_text: Check platform attachment bolts and pins

defect.title: Fastener defects found during major structural inspection
defect.description: Fastener defects found that require correction before unit can be returned to service.

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

guidance: Detailed inspection of hydraulic system components
Especially if hydraulic work was performed
Check all hydraulic cylinders for leaks, mounting, and rod condition
Inspect all hydraulic hoses and fittings
Check control valves and manifolds
Verify relief valve settings per manufacturer specifications
Test holding valves/load checks (will be tested under load)
Check hydraulic fluid level and condition

fields.label: Was hydraulic work part of the major repair/modification?
Hydraulic Components Replaced (if applicable)
fields.help_text: List cylinders, hoses, valves, etc. that were replaced

fields.label: All Hydraulic Cylinders (Leaks, Mounting, Rod Condition)
Relief Valve Settings Verified
fields.help_text: Relief valves set per manufacturer specifications

fields.label: Holding Valves/Load Checks Tested
fields.help_text: Holding valves prevent drift - will be tested under load

fields.label: Hydraulic Fluid Condition and Level
fields.help_text: Check fluid level and condition (contamination, etc.)

defect.title: Hydraulic system defects found during major structural inspection
defect.description: Hydraulic system defects found that require correction before unit can be returned to service.

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

guidance: Complete functional test of all systems per Section 8.2.5
Test every control from both ground and platform positions
Test E-stop, ground override, manual lowering
Test all interlocks, warnings, boom angle indicators, etc.
Check that all boom movements are smooth and controlled
Listen for grinding, squeaking, or other abnormal sounds

fields.label: All Operating Controls Tested (Ground and Platform)
fields.help_text: Test every control from both positions

fields.label: All Emergency Controls Tested
fields.help_text: Test E-stop, ground override, manual lowering

fields.label: All Safety Devices Tested
fields.help_text: Test interlocks, warnings, boom angle indicators, etc.

fields.label: All Boom Movements Smooth and Controlled
fields.help_text: No binding, jerking, or hesitation in any movement

fields.label: Any unusual noises during operation?
fields.help_text: Listen for grinding, squeaking, or other abnormal sounds

fields.label: Functional Testing Notes
fields.help_text: Document any abnormalities or concerns

defect.title: Functional defects found during major structural inspection
defect.description: Functional defects found that require correction before unit can be returned to service.

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

fields.label: Load test performed?
fields.help_text: REQUIRED after major structural work per ANSI A92.2

fields.label: Load Test Date
fields.help_text: If performed separately, enter date

fields.label: Load Test Result: PASS
fields.help_text: Load test must pass before return to service

fields.label: Load Test Report Reference
fields.help_text: Reference to separate load test inspection or report

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

fields.label: Is this an insulated aerial device?
Is dielectric test required for this work?
fields.help_text: Required if insulating components were repaired or modified

fields.label: Dielectric Test Report Reference
fields.help_text: Reference to separate dielectric test inspection

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

guidance: Verify all markings, placards, and required documentation are present and current
If modifications affect capacity, new chart required
Modifications must be documented with placard per Section 7.11
Operating manual must reflect any modifications made

fields.label: Capacity Chart Current and Accurate
fields.help_text: If modifications affect capacity, new chart required

fields.label: Modification Placard (if modifications were made)
fields.help_text: Modifications must be documented with placard per Section 7.11

fields.label: Operating Manual Updated for Modifications
fields.help_text: Manual must reflect any modifications made

fields.label: All Warning Labels Present and Legible
Any marking or documentation deficiencies found?

defect.title: Marking or documentation deficiencies found
defect.description: Markings, placards, or documentation deficiencies found that require correction.

standard_text.excerpt: Markings on the aerial device shall not be removed, defaced, or altered. All missing or illegible markings shall be promptly replaced.

fields.label: Major structural work completed per specifications?
No structural defects or safety issues found?
fields.help_text: All defects corrected and verified

fields.label: All required tests passed (functional, load, dielectric if applicable)?
All repair/modification documentation complete?
fields.help_text: Work orders, procedures, certifications, test results

fields.label: Equipment is ready for return to service?
fields.help_text: Check YES only if ALL requirements met

fields.label: Any operational restrictions or limitations?
fields.help_text: Document any restrictions on operation

fields.label: Final Evaluation Summary
fields.help_text: Comprehensive summary of inspection findings and recommendations

standard_text.excerpt: A major structural inspection of the mobile unit (MEWP) shall be performed after the first fifteen years of service, and at every ten year interval thereafter.

fields.label: Comprehensive inspection report completed with all findings documented
All repair/modification records attached to inspection file
All test results attached (load test, dielectric test, NDT results)
Photographs of repaired areas and critical components documented
Any required engineering approvals or calculations filed
Any manufacturer correspondence or approvals filed
Inspection decal applied with date and next inspection due
Customer provided complete inspection report and documentation package
All records filed in permanent equipment file per Section 7.11

standard_text.excerpt: Markings on the aerial device shall not be removed, defaced, or altered. All missing or illegible markings shall be promptly replaced.

rules.title: Load test is REQUIRED after major structural work

on_fail.defect_title: Load test not performed after major structural work
on_fail.defect_description: ANSI A92.2 requires load test after major repairs or modifications. Unit cannot be returned to service without load test.

rules.title: Load test must pass before return to service

on_fail.defect_description: Load test must pass before unit can be returned to service.

rules.title: All repairs and modifications must be properly documented

on_fail.defect_description: All repairs and modifications must be documented per Section 7.11 before unit can be returned to service.

rules.title: No structural defects remaining after inspection

on_fail.defect_title: Structural defects found during major structural inspection
on_fail.defect_description: Structural defects remain that require correction before unit can be returned to service.
All required tests (functional, load, dielectric if applicable) must pass before unit can be returned to service.
